source_jobs.title: AnaVation General Consideration
source_jobs.description:
Be Challenged and Make a Difference 
 
In a world of technology, people make the difference. We believe if we invest in great people, then great things will happen. At AnaVation, we provide unmatched value to our customers and employees through innovative solutions and an engaging culture. 


AnaVation is the leader in solving the most complex technical challenges for collection and processing in the U.S. Federal Intelligence Community.  AnaVation delivers groundbreaking research, and advanced software and systems engineering that provides an information advantage contributing to the mission and operational success of our customers. AnaVation is a US owned company headquartered in Chantilly, Virginia.  We provide our employees with competitive compensation packages and a full range of benefits, including vacation, medical, dental, life insurance, a 401(k) plan, tuition assistance, Employee Assistance Program (EAP), and paid parental leave.

Don't see the perfect opportunity at this time but still want to connect with us?

Complete an application and submit your resume so we can stay in touch!.

We looking forward to connecting with you as we continue to grow.

source_jobs.title: Application Administrator
source_jobs.description:
Be Challenged and Make a Difference 
 
In a world of technology, people make the difference. We believe if we invest in great people, then great things will happen. At AnaVation, we provide unmatched value to our customers and employees through innovative solutions and an engaging culture. 


AnaVation is seeking an Application Administrator to join our team and support our mission critical Government customer in Hanover, MD. The position assists in resolving Tier 3 incidents, performs routine ServiceNow administration tasks, and supports workflow and reporting applications. In this role, the selected candidate will report to the Operations Lead and collaborate closely with developers, data engineers, and the Agile Scrum Master. The ideal candidate will work across three different enclaves or networks in a mission-critical, on-site environment, with the opportunity to collaborate with cross-functional teams and gain hand-on ServiceNow workflow, dashboard, reporting, and analytics experience.
Key Responsibilities:
Provide Tier 3 Application support to customer workflows, reporting, dashboards, and ServiceNow automation.
Install, configure, and maintain application and platform components
Monitor application performance, identify issues, and perform troubleshooting ensure system stability.
Perform platform updates, patches, and upgrades.
Provide technical support to end-users, including resolving access and functional issues.
Create and manage role-based user accounts and maintain appropriate access controls.
Manage application licenses and support compliance requirement.
Support user training and provide guidance on new functionality and best practices.

source_jobs.title: Incident Response/Security Analyst - Temporary
source_jobs.description:
Be Challenged and Make a Difference 
 
In a world of technology, people make the difference. We believe if we invest in great people, then great things will happen. At AnaVation, we provide unmatched value to our customers and employees through innovative solutions and an engaging culture. 


Description of Task to be Performed:    
AnaVation is seeking an Incident Response/Security Analyst (Temporary Role) to help our mission-critical customer in Washington, DC.
Responsibilities:
Create, track, monitor and investigate security related events/incidents through closure.
Monitor, maintain and administer policies and rules within EDR and SIEM tools (e.g., Crowdstrike, Splunk).
Participate in or lead the remediation of incidents and responses that are generated from live threats against the enterprise.
Perform incident response analysis based on investigation requirements.
Support and develop reports during and after incidents, which include all actions taken to properly mitigate, recover and return operations to normal operations.
Assist in developing and implementing defensive cyber best practice tactics, techniques, and procedures.
Assist in conducting vulnerability scans using Tenable SC and Nessus Manager.  Manage the applications and conduct vulnerability analysis.
Maintain Incident Ticketing tracking system and related tickets within Remedy.
Monitor and take action within multiple tools providing security functions such as vulnerability management (e.g., Nessus), configuration management (e.g., Tenable Security Center, IBM BigFix, SCCM, McAfee ePO), endpoint protection (e.g., antivirus, ATP), intrusion detection software and hardware.
Perform Splunk queries to examine and query log data from the Enterprise Logging as a Service system.
Interacting with GRC tool (e.g., CSAM) to perform daily/weekly vulnerability analysis.
Creating and compiling weekly security metrics into dashboards and charts.
Flexible with other security related tasks as needed by the customer.
This position is on-site in Washington, DC.

source_jobs.title: Information Assurance Specialist
source_jobs.description:
Be Challenged and Make a Difference 
 
In a world of technology, people make the difference. We believe if we invest in great people, then great things will happen. At AnaVation, we provide unmatched value to our customers and employees through innovative solutions and an engaging culture. 


Description of Task to be Performed: 
The Information Assurance/Security Engineer will provide security engineering and information assurance support to the Government’s Assessment and Authorization (A&A) process to maintain Authority to Operate (ATO) and Authority to Connect (ATC) for mission applications and services. Designs, develops, monitors and documents security controls, security testing, security reporting, and plan of actions and milestones (POA&Ms) throughout systems and application lifecycles in support of mission systems.
Provides security engineering design inputs, security design reviews, and security best practices as part of technical and
change requests.
Configures and validates secure systems, physical controls, and tests security products and systems to detect security
weaknesses.
Maintains XACTA security records for supported systems.

source_jobs.title: ISSO - Information Systems Security Officer
source_jobs.description:
Description of Task to be Performed:

AnaVation is seeking an Information System Security Officer (ISSO) to support the security posture of systems, applications, and networks. In this role, the ISSO will apply current Information Assurance (IA) technologies to the architecture, design, development, evaluation, and integration of enterprise environments to ensure compliance with Federal Information Security Modernization Act (FISMA) requirements and applicable security standards.

The ideal candidate will advise the Government on the use of security methods and technologies, including encryption, vulnerability analysis, and security management standards, to protect Government systems and applications. This role requires close coordination with program leadership, infrastructure teams, developers, and other security stakeholders to maintain compliance, support RMF activities, and sustain the overall system security posture.

Key Responsibilities / Skills

  • Apply current Information Assurance (IA) technologies to maintain and improve the security posture of systems, applications, and networks.
  • Advise the Government on security methods and controls, including encryption technologies, vulnerability analysis, and security management standards, to support FISMA compliance.
  • Communicate security requirements clearly and accurately through strong verbal and written communication, including documentation within required security artifacts and RMF systems.
  • Ensure annual FISMA deadlines are met, and notify the Government PM when deadlines are at risk or assistance is needed. 
  • Prepare and maintain security documentation from approved templates, including:
  • Configuration Management Plan (CMP)
  • Incident Response Plan (IRP)
  • Information System Contingency Plan (ISCP)
  • Ensure documentation complies with FBI Policy Directives (PDs), Policy Guides (PGs), and Federal IA requirements, and coordinate required reviews and approvals. 
  • Evaluate program policies and procedures, identify security or compliance gaps, and elevate issues to management for resolution. 
  • Identify IA vulnerabilities and coordinate with Infrastructure and Development teams to remediate, mitigate, or document exceptions through the POA&M process.
  • Review vulnerability findings, patches, updates, and compliance scan results, including SCAP and DISA STIG assessments, to ensure systems and applications remain compliant in both on-premises and cloud environments.
  • Prepare and maintain Security Authorization packages to obtain and sustain an Authority to Operate (ATO), Authority to Test (ATT), or other authorization types for systems and applications.
  • Attend Configuration Control Board (CCB) meetings and review change requests for impact to system and application security posture, Federal compliance requirements, and FBI PD/PG requirements; document outcomes in the CMP. 
  • Coordinate security incident response activities and high-priority compliance responses with the FBI Enterprise Security Operations Center (ESOC).
  • Represent program security interests in internal and external meetings with stakeholders, customers, and partner organizations.
  • Schedule and lead meetings with program personnel to address findings, determine remediation paths, and document outcomes within the CMP and POA&M as needed. 
  • Coordinate with other system ISSOs to ensure interconnection requirements, policies, procedures, and documentation are properly addressed and maintained.
  • Assess current and emerging security threats within an operational environment and provide recommendations to reduce risk. 

This position requires active Top Secret (TS) clearance and the ability to obtain SCI access with a CI

source_jobs.description:

In a world of technology, people make the difference. We believe if we invest in great people, then great things will happen. At AnaVation, we provide unmatched value to our customers and employees through innovative solutions and an engaging culture. 


Description of Task to be Performed: 
AnaVation is seeking a Lead Software Developer with an active Secret clearance. The ideal candidate will be a technical and thought leader with a passion for modernizing legacy enterprise applications in the Federal government space. This is a full-time position located onsite with our customer in Washington DC.
What you will be doing
Provide technical, thought, and people leadership in the redesign, development, maintenance, and modernization of a large, multi-tenant, legacy software application, including hands-on development support as needed
Resolve particularly complex technical issues; provide innovative technical solutions to address environmental constraints and challenges
Develop and maintain stakeholder relationships to support activities throughout the entire SDLC
Ensure attainment of all SLAs/SLOs in accordance with the QASP
Mentor cross-functional development team members to achieve collective success
Must have the ability to work in a dynamic environment and adapt to changing conditions
Must possess a high degree of originality, creativity, and initiative requiring minimal supervision

source_jobs.title: Lead Systems Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Be Challenged and Make a Difference 
 
In a world of technology, people make the difference. We believe if we invest in great people, then great things will happen. At AnaVation, we provide unmatched value to our customers and employees through innovative solutions and an engaging culture. 


Description of Task to be Performed: AnaVation is seeking a Lead Systems Engineer to join our team and support our mission critical customer in Reston, VA. As Lead, you will represent the engineering team in customer meetings, plan quarterly Agile Development Planning commitments, drive technology roadmaps, work with the AnaVation Program Manager to accelerate customer priorities, and mentor senior, mid-level, and junior engineers. This team works with AWS cloud services including modernization of Elastic, RHEL, WSUS, and cross domain integration and automation. Our team supports and enhances 10 core application and data services used by multiple Government Agencies.  The Engineering Team includes 15 staff (Systems Integration Engineers, Cloud Engineers, Software Engineers, System Administrators, Database Administrators, Data Scientists, and Information System Security Officers).  
 
This position is responsible for planning/estimating staffing, software licensing, and hosting capacity for roadmap projects and capabilities. 
 
This position is also a personnel supervisor position with 5-7 direct reports including performance reviews, engineering career planning, technical interviews for new staff, Agile Delivery (Jira) prioritization and reviews, and ongoing timesheet approvals.

source_jobs.description:
Be Challenged and Make a Difference 
 
In a world of technology, people make the difference. We believe if we invest in great people, then great things will happen. At AnaVation, we provide unmatched value to our customers and employees through innovative solutions and an engaging culture. 


Description of Task to be Performed:

AnaVation is seeking an experienced Litigation Support Engineer to provide support to a mission critical customer. The selected candidate will be responsible for the administration, optimization, support, and usage of enterprise e-Litigation systems across multiple network enclaves to ensure reliable, secure, and efficient access to case-related data. The ideal candidate has a background in IT as well as experience with litigation support.

Key Responsibilities
· Administer and maintain litigation support platforms (e.g., Relativity, IPRO Eclipse, Everlaw, Nuix, Lexmark) across multiple enclaves.
· Perform queries using these systems and others; perform analysis of information sources, timeline development, communication patterns and data quality controls.
· Develop data dashboards within case repositories (e.g., Relativity or Everlaw) and/or using COTS data analysis tools (e.g. Power BI) to support case team review.

This position is on site with the customer in Washington, DC and cannot be supported remotely. 
The selected candidate must possess an active TS clearance with the ability to obtain SCI accesses. Candidates without an active TS cannot be considered.

source_jobs.title: Program Manager (App Dev)
source_jobs.description:
Be Challenged and Make a Difference 
 
In a world of technology, people make the difference. We believe if we invest in great people, then great things will happen. At AnaVation, we provide unmatched value to our customers and employees through innovative solutions and an engaging culture. 


Description of Task to be Performed: 
AnaVation is seeking a senior-level Program Manager with an active Secret clearance. The ideal candidate will have Federal Program Management experience with teams of at least 20 personnel plus hands-on experience leading Application Development and Modernization activities. This is a full-time position located onsite with our customer in Washington DC.
What you will be doing
Fully accountable for planning, organizing, and executing all aspects of program scope, schedule, cost, technical, and staffing performance and activities on complex application development contracts to ensure exceptional service delivery
Manage subcontractors to ensure their strict adherence to AnaVation and client standards of quality and performance; present a “one team” view to the client
Ensure timely, complete, high-quality contract deliverables; ensure SLOs are met or exceeded 100% of the time
Interface with client’s senior management personnel, including briefings up to CIO/CISO level
Support IT Security Audits including FISMA, FISCAM, IRS, and OIG
Leverages industry knowledge, best practices, lessons learned and stakeholder feedback to develop, implement and continuously improve application services
Optimize processes to maximize efficiencies within the team and program, including functional integration across task areas and teams
Be a positive change agent, both within and outside the organization; propagate our corporate culture throughout the program
Lead risk management activities, including identification and recommended mitigations; track and manage risks and issues through closure
Build, grow, and develop high-performing teams aligned with the client’s mission and strategy
Manage program personnel to include interviewing, hiring, performance evaluations, compensation, and training and development
Guide and mentor subordinate managers, team leads, and staff 
Work closely with the client to function as the single point of contact and accountability, ensuring that all technical work, communications, and decision-making remain aligned, timely, and defensible
Serve as Quality Control Manager ensuring all deliverables and assigned tasks meet QASP requirements
Must have the ability to work in a dynamic environment and adapt to changing conditions
Must possess a high degree of originality, creativity, and initiative requiring minimal supervision

AnaVation is seeking a detail-oriented and proactive Program Support Specialist to provide administrative and operational support located at Fort Belvoir, VA. The ideal candidate will be a versatile and organized individual capable of managing multiple tasks while ensuring the smooth execution of daily operations and special events. This role requires excellent communication skills, the ability to handle sensitive information, and a commitment to supporting the mission.  
 
Responsibilities include:
Track, route, and monitor taskers; ensure deadlines are met; maintain accurate documentation and status updates.
Plan, schedule, and support meetings, ceremonies, briefings, and special events; manage logistics, materials, and on-site execution.
Coordinate and support engagements involving international partners; assist with scheduling, protocol considerations, documentation, and logistics for multinational meetings, visits, and collaborative events.
Perform a wide range of administrative duties, including monitoring deadlines, tracking action items, and ensuring timely completion of assigned tasks
Monitor and refine work products, processes, and workflows as necessary.
Proactively identify actions and provide accurate, professional assessments.
Research information and prepare reports and/or briefs.
Provide customer service tasks for senior leadership.

source_jobs.title: Scrum Master
source_jobs.description:
Be Challenged and Make a Difference 
 
In a world of technology, people make the difference. We believe if we invest in great people, then great things will happen. At AnaVation, we provide unmatched value to our customers and employees through innovative solutions and an engaging culture. 


AnaVation is seeking a Scrum Master to join our team and support our mission critical Government customer in Hanover, MD.  The Scrum Master facilitates SAFe delivery practices for cross-functional operations and engineering teams supporting software development, enhancements, and Tier 3 help desk operations in a large-scale, multi-tenant enterprise environment. The role is a servant leader and agile coach who guides the team in delivering maximum business value within the Scaled Agile Framework. The ideal candidate will be successful at removing complex roadblocks and issues while facilitating team-level execution, and managing dependencies and risks during PI planning, while remaining aligned with enterprise goals.
Key Responsibilities:
Facilitate SAFe Agile ceremonies, including sprint planning, standups, reviews, retrospectives, and PI Planning.
Coordinate work across operations, engineering, and support teams.
Track and help resolve risks, dependencies, and impediments.
Support backlog management, sprint execution, and operational improvements.
Communicate delivery status, metrics, and risks to stakeholders.
Promote Agile best practices and continuous improvement.

Description of Task to be Performed:  AnaVation is looking for a talented Senior Cyber Advisor who is passionate about supporting National Security missions. The ideal candidate appreciates partnering with our customer and a group of cybersecurity experts to advise senior personnel in support of military intelligence missions.  This position is full time on-site Annapolis Junction, Maryland.
 
Position Responsibilities: Provides high level guidance and strategic advice in Cyber/Intelligence planning and innovation, ensuring alignment with organizational goals and advancements. Review and assess current capabilities, identify gaps, and deliver actionable recommendations to enhance Cyber/Intelligence operations and tradecraft. Assess customer needs to determine and articulate functional requirements for complex systems, including information systems architecture, networking, and other related domains and develop plans to address these needs.

source_jobs.title: Senior Proposal Manager
source_jobs.description:
Be Challenged and Make a Difference 
 
In a world of technology, people make the difference. We believe if we invest in great people, then great things will happen. At AnaVation, we provide unmatched value to our customers and employees through innovative solutions and an engaging culture. 


AnaVation is seeking a hands-on Senior Proposal Manager to augment our existing Business Development Team. We are looking for a versatile, high-impact team player who can wear multiple hats and contribute across various functions. The ideal candidate is a utility player—someone who thrives in a dynamic environment, seamlessly stepping in to manage proposals, coordinate teams, and support critical business development efforts as needed. If you have a proven track record of leading successful federal proposals that result in awarded contracts and you meet or exceed the qualifications below, we want to speak with you.
 
The Senior Proposal Manager is primarily responsible for managing and writing proposals in support of AnaVation’s growth goals.  The candidate will work closely with senior leadership, capture/business owners, other proposal managers, engineers, proposal coordinator, and operations staff. 
 
The Proposal Manager oversees all proposal activities from inception to submission, including managing proposal schedules and deadlines, developing proposal outlines and compliance matrices, writing of proposal content, and monitoring the progress of proposals. Role involves implementation of all necessary proposal procedures and processes. This is a highly networked role that requires an ability to work under pressure and meet challenging deadlines.
 
Position Responsibilities Include:
Deliver compliant, professionally produced proposals within defined timeframes
Participate in the development and review of proposal strategies and themes
Direct the creation and review of proposal outlines that will be used as a key document design and control medium
Leads and participates in collaborative sessions to refine win strategy, competitive assessments, and customer pain points or hot buttons.
Assist in analyzing solicitation requirements against proposal outlines to ensure compliance and responsiveness to the customer's issues and needs
Develop and conduct all color reviews and follow up for internal evaluation of the technical, management, and overall proposal approaches
Write and edit proposals for continuity, logic, organization, messaging to enhance delivery and AnaVation differentiators
Coordinate and review / edit proposal input from a variety of stakeholders. Specifically working with internal solutions architects, SMEs, and teaming partners
Maintain proposal files, past performance catalogue; create and maintain corporate SharePoint proposal site for each managed opportunity.
Serve as an author or proposal section and develop RFI responses 
 
This is a hybrid role in Chantilly,VA. Relocation assistance is not available for this position.

Be Challenged and Make a Difference 
 
In a world of technology, people make the difference. We believe if we invest in great people, then great things will happen. At AnaVation, we provide unmatched value to our customers and employees through innovative solutions and an engaging culture. 


AnaVation is seeking a ServiceNow Developer to join our team and support our mission critical Government customer in Hanover, MD.  In this role you will design, develop, and maintain robust ServiceNow applications and workflows supporting enterprise operations, including workflow and workload management, reporting and analytics, and system integrations. As a key technical contributor, you will work closely with stakeholders, business analysts, data engineers, and architects to translate mission requirements into scalable, secure, and compliant technical solutions. Due to the nature of the work performed, candidates must demonstrate a strong understanding of security controls within a federal enterprise environment.
Key Responsibilities:
Develop and configure ServiceNow applications, workflows, and workload management solutions using JavaScript, including reports, dashboards, and analytics.
Implement enhancements to existing ServiceNow functionality.
Develop and support REST/SOAP API integrations using JSON and XML.
Serve as Tier 3 support to troubleshoot and resolve application, workflow, and integration issues.
Effectively communicate and collaborate with cross-functional teams to deliver solutions.
Support data validation, transformation, and migration activities.

source_jobs.description:
Be Challenged and Make a Difference 
 
In a world of technology, people make the difference. We believe if we invest in great people, then great things will happen. At AnaVation, we provide unmatched value to our customers and employees through innovative solutions and an engaging culture. 


AnaVation is seeking an ServiceNow Operations Lead to join our team and support our mission critical Government customer in Hanover, MD. This is an impactful role that provides leadership and direction for support of a ServiceNow workflow and reporting environment including Tier 3 Help Desk operations.
In this role, the selected candidate will report to the Program Manager and oversee enterprise platform stability, workflow execution, and service delivery performance.  The position provides coordination and oversight for Agile activities supported by the Scrum Master. The ideal candidate will work in a strictly regulated federal government environment, providing on-site presence for close collaboration with government stakeholders and cross-functional teams.
Key Responsibilities:
Provide leadership and direction for Tier 3 Systems Administrator support operations within an enterprise service management platform, ensuring effective delivery of advanced technical support and service operations.
Manage escalation handling and resolution of complex incidents and operational issues within the ServiceNow environment.
Oversee daily operational workflows, including workload prioritization, service delivery performance, and adherence to service level agreements (SLAs).
Ensure platform stability and reliability, supporting cloud-based infrastructure, system integrations, and data migration activities.
Monitor and report on operational risks, issues, dependencies, and key performance metrics, leveraging analytics to drive continuous service improvement.

source_jobs.description:
Be Challenged and Make a Difference 
 
In a world of technology, people make the difference. We believe if we invest in great people, then great things will happen. At AnaVation, we provide unmatched value to our customers and employees through innovative solutions and an engaging culture. 


Description of Task to be Performed:
AnaVation is seeking an experienced SIEM (Security Information and Event Management) Engineer to provide support to a mission critical customer. The selected candidate will be responsible for the design, deployment, configuration, and maintenance of SIEM systems across multiple classification enclaves.

Key Responsibilities
· Support the architecture, engineering, optimization, and sustainment of Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) platforms supporting defensive cyber operations, advanced threat detection, incident response, and compliance initiatives
· Perform configuration, management and maintenance of network firewalls, security and encryption devices, including IDS, NAC and SIEM systems.
· Integrate diverse log sources including firewalls, IDS/IPS, EDR, servers, network devices, security appliances, and cloud environments.
· Optimize SIEM performance, storage architecture, data retention policies, and system scalability.

This position is on site with the customer in Washington, DC and cannot be supported remotely. 
The selected candidate must possess an active TS clearance with the ability to obtain SCI accesses. Candidates
without an active TS cannot be considered.

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer - Full Stack Developer
source_jobs.description:
Description of Task to be Performed:
AnaVation is looking for a talented Software Developer who is passionate about technology and working with cutting edge software to support our mission-critical hybrid cloud big data program. The ideal candidate appreciates partnering with our customer and a group of engineers to create capabilities to support cybersecurity missions. This position is on-site work based in San Antonio, TX.
 
Our team of engineers is working with cutting edge technologies to create innovative solutions that meet our customers ever-growing requirements. We have the ability to research, define, and recommend technologies to our customer and are involved in all parts of the software development life cycle. The applications we help to develop are highly customized, help to automate the business processes of our customer, and allow them to perform various types of analysis on datasets that they would not normally be able to do. Our engineers are continually learning and setting the standards for our customers. If you are looking to be challenged and grow as a Software Developer, then this is the environment for you!
